easy.question: What is the core purpose of the Mobility Scooter Policy and does it apply everywhere?
easy.answer: To manage the risks from storing, charging and using mobility scooters/powered wheelchairs so people and buildings remain safe (especially for fire/evacuation). It applies to all Midland Heart properties and service users. :contentReference[oaicite:18]{index=18}

easy.question: Is written permission required to store/charge a scooter in a Midland Heart building?
easy.answer: Yes. Unless Midland Heart grants written permission (following risk assessment), no vehicle may be stored, used or charged in a property. :contentReference[oaicite:19]{index=19}

easy.question: What is a designated mobility scooter storage area and name two mandatory features.
easy.answer: It’s a specific space set aside solely for scooter storage. It must be lockable and clearly labelled, provide fire separation from escape routes, have automatic fire detection, suitable electrics for charging, and natural ventilation. :contentReference[oaicite:20]{index=20}

easy.question: Which scooters are ‘Class 3’ and can they be stored in flats/communal areas?
easy.answer: Class 3 are road-legal scooters up to 8 mph (restricted to 4 mph on pedestrian routes), max 150 kg, DVLA-registered and age 14+. They must not be stored in flats/communal areas unless there’s a designated scooter store (Mutuals/LSE consider case-by-case). :contentReference[oaicite:21]{index=21}

easy.question: What happens with Class 3 scooters already in situ as of June 2024?
easy.answer: They can remain only if a risk assessment is in place and the scooter can be stored safely. :contentReference[oaicite:22]{index=22}

easy.question: How is compliance checked over time?
easy.answer: Through regular monitoring, including weekly/monthly housekeeping and fire safety checks, periodic home checks, FRA reviews and senior manager audits. :contentReference[oaicite:23]{index=23}

medium.question: A tenant asks to store and charge their scooter. Outline the permission route and ongoing conditions, referring to the process diagram.
medium.answer: Follow the request process in the *flowchart on page 3*: waiting list if needed; complete the Scooter Storage Questionnaire; issue/sign a Licence Agreement and Direct Debit Mandate; Income set up a PER sub-account and apply a £15 PCM charge; ensure key/fob access; RLO/SM conduct daily spot checks and 6-monthly audits; non-payment triggers notices; termination requires cancelling the DD. :contentReference[oaicite:24]{index=24}

medium.question: In a Retirement Living Mutual, who is consulted on storage refusals and options?
medium.answer: Relevant committee members are consulted on storage, refusals and alternatives; Class 3 requests are presented to the committee case-by-case. :contentReference[oaicite:25]{index=25}

medium.question: A resident wants to keep a scooter in a corridor ‘just overnight’. Why is this not acceptable?
medium.answer: Communal areas must remain clear for escape routes under the Fire Safety Order; scooters in corridors materially increase fire/egress risk. Permission requires a compliant storage area and written approval. :contentReference[oaicite:26]{index=26}

medium.question: What reasonable adjustments can be offered during the application process?
medium.answer: Communication/format adjustments are agreed case-by-case (no fixed list), with minimal delay; expert bodies may be consulted to support the tenant. :contentReference[oaicite:27]{index=27}

medium.question: If a tenant stops paying the licence fee, what management actions follow?
medium.answer: Per the *flowchart on page 3*, Income addresses missed DDs; notices may be served by Midland Heart or the tenant can serve an NTQ to end the licence. :contentReference[oaicite:28]{index=28}

hard.question: A scheme without a designated store has rising scooter demand (including Class 3). Draft a safe interim and long-term plan.
hard.answer: Interim: prohibit storage/charging in flats/communal areas without permission; complete individual risk assessments; offer personal storage alternatives off-site where feasible; communicate Fire Safety Order duties. Long-term: assess feasibility and design of a designated store that is lockable, fire-separated, detected, adequately ventilated with safe electrical supply; consult committees in Mutuals/LSE; implement a licence/charging regime with inspections and audits. :contentReference[oaicite:29]{index=29}

hard.question: Two tenants insist on charging scooters in a lounge because the store sockets are full. How should risk and policy be applied?
hard.answer: Charging in communal lounges breaches the requirement to use designated storage/charging areas with fire separation and detection. Increase capacity in the store or schedule charging to avoid overloading; enforce the policy and housekeeping checks. :contentReference[oaicite:30]{index=30}

hard.question: A Class 3 scooter owner demands an exception on disability grounds. Balance Equality Act considerations with building safety.
hard.answer: Consider reasonable adjustments to *access* and *process*, but not at the expense of core life-safety controls. Where storage is unsafe or non-compliant, identify compliant alternatives (e.g., external store) or route through Mutuals/LSE committee case-by-case; document rationale under the Fire Safety Order and building regs. :contentReference[oaicite:31]{index=31}

hard.question: What evidence and records should be kept to show ongoing safe scooter management at a scheme?
hard.answer: Licence agreements and DD mandates; waiting list and questionnaire; access control logs; daily spot checks; six-monthly audit forms; FRA references; non-payment notices; and any committee decisions (Mutuals/LSE). :contentReference[oaicite:32]{index=32}

hard.question: After a near-miss in a corridor, what immediate and systemic actions should the scheme take?
hard.answer: Immediately remove scooters from communal routes; re-brief residents; re-inspect housekeeping and fire safety checks; review the risk assessment; consider creating or improving a designated store; and record actions for senior manager audit. :contentReference[oaicite:33]{index=33}
